# CircleCI CLI

Mark this project as free and open source (OSS).

OSS projects receive extra free build minutes on CircleCI and are
visible on the public builds page. Enable this only for genuinely
open-source repositories.

JSON fields: is_oss

For more information about output formatting flags, see `circleci help formatting`.

## Usage

`circleci project settings oss [flags]`

## Flags

| Flag               | Description                                             |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--disable`        | Disable the setting                                     |
| `--enable`         | Enable the setting                                      |
| `--jq string`      | Process values from the response using jq syntax        |
| `--json`           | Output as JSON                                          |
| `--project string` | Project slug (e.g. gh/org/repo); defaults to git remote |

## Inherited Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                  |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `-c, --config string` | Path to config file (default: ~/.config/circleci/config.yml) |
| `--debug`             | Enable debug logging                                         |
| `--no-color`          | Disable ANSI color output (same as setting NO_COLOR)         |
| `-q, --quiet`         | Suppress informational output. Data on stdout is unaffected  |

## Examples

- Show current value and pick a new one interactively (TTY): 
  `circleci project settings oss`
- Show current value for a specific project (non-interactive): 
  `circleci project settings oss --project gh/myorg/myrepo`
- Enable the setting directly (non-interactive / scripting): 
  `circleci project settings oss --enable`
- Disable the setting directly (non-interactive / scripting): 
  `circleci project settings oss --disable`
- Output the current value as JSON: 
  `circleci project settings oss --json`

## Learn More

- Use `circleci <command> <subcommand> --help` for more information about a command.
- Read the manual at <https://cli.circleci.com/reference/>
- Support at <https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/issues>
